content: Existing Self-Refine frameworks, which generate iterative self-feedback without extra training or supervision, can be structurally enhanced by integrating multiple inconsistent solving perspectives from LLM self-reflection mechanisms to break the confirmation bias that occurs when an LLM only refines its own initial output. This hybrid approach retains the core advantage of Self-Refine (no additional training or external data) while fixing the common failure mode where iterative self-refinement gets stuck in local optima.
